question_text: Which of the following forces acts between objects that are not in physical contact?

options.label: A
options.text: Air resistance acting on a moving car

options.label: B
options.text: The magnetic force between a magnet and an iron nail held nearby

options.label: C
options.text: The tension in a rope being pulled

options.label: D
options.text: The force exerted by a person pushing a shopping trolley

correct_answer: The magnetic force between a magnet and an iron nail held nearby
explanation: Non-contact forces act between objects without them touching. Magnetic force is a non-contact force that can attract or repel objects across a distance. Air resistance, rope tension, and pushing forces all require direct physical contact.
